title: Berry Flux Gives Node Charge
summary: Once the occupied states are organized into a line bundle, the flux of Berry curvature through a small sphere around a bad point yields the node's integer topological charge.
assumptions: The sphere encloses only one bad point.
The occupied bundle is regular on the punctured sphere.
regime: Berry-geometric analysis of isolated Weyl nodes.
scope: Lecture One curvature-based derivation of node charge.

source_anchors.notes: The derivation uses the Berry bundle and its curvature.

representation: Geometric derivation from curvature flux to integer node charge.
failure_modes: The abelian derivation needs modification for multiple occupied bands.
retrieval_hints: Use for the geometric route from Berry data to Weyl-node charge.
